violations.text: The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was 500 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quaternary ammonia sanitizer is 150-400 ppm. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Aluminum wrap observed lining kitchen shelves. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
The ceiling vent in the 3 bay sink area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

Raw eggs observed held at 60-65 degrees Fahrenheit. Raw eggs must be held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
Pork was observed with date marks exceeding 7 days (March 1 - March 5, 2026) Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pork was placed hot in the walk in cooler in containers greater than four inches deep. Pork was divided into additional containers. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

Pans in the dishwashing area observed not in good repair, one with melted plastic chipping off the bottom, and one very dented, impacting their ability to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
One ceiling vent in the 3 bay sink area observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

A container was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Seafood salad was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in freezer for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked meat was placed hot in the walk-in cooler in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC put meat in 2 inch containers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025
Several hand-washing sink in the kitchen area were lacking proper hand-washing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC disposed of ice and took unit out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Batter and pooled eggs were observed held between 68-69 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed items on ice. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025
Garlic/onion blend and vegetable tempura were observed exceeding 7 day date marking. PIC disposed of items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The cooking utensils were observed stored on an unclean surface (rags) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC removed rag and washed/sanitized items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Raw animal foods (such as beef and eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as veggies) in the walk-in refrigerator and reach-in refrigerators. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be (200-400) ppm. PIC informed to manually mix sanitizer and test sanitizer until dispenser is repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Milk at barista cooler was observed held at (50) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food that was out of temperature for an unknown amount of time was voluntarily disposed of. Unit taken out of service to hold TCS/PHF foods during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
